extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is violated by rule r3. Enum order low-to-high is: self_care < routine < urgent < emergent.

Compare two inputs differing only in vomiting, with abdominal_pain=true and pallor=leg_swelling=confusion=false.

BEFORE (vomiting=false): Only r2 (IF abdominal_pain THEN urgent, priority 2) matches. r3 requires vomiting and does not fire. Default not needed. Winner = urgent.

AFTER (vomiting=true, the witness assignment): r2 (urgent, priority 2) and r3 (IF abdominal_pain AND vomiting THEN self_care, priority 100) both match. The highest-priority matching rule wins, so r3 (priority 100) overrides r2. Winner = self_care.

Setting vomiting to true changed acuity from urgent to self_care, which is LOWER. No other rule (r1/r4/r5) fires because pallor, leg_swelling, and confusion are all false, so r3 is the uncontested highest-priority match in the after-state. This directly contradicts the property that setting vomiting true never lowers acuity. The witness assignment given is the AFTER input (vomiting=true) that yields self_care, demonstrably below the urgent it would produce with vomiting=false.
